label: March 16, 2026
lead_title: Automation is rewriting paid social operations — and creative plus signal quality are becoming the real levers.
lead_summary: Across Meta, TikTok, Reddit, Pinterest, Snap, X, and YouTube / Google Ads, the trend is clear: less manual campaign architecture, more model-led delivery, more blended commerce and creator workflows, and more pressure on leadership teams to explain measurement shifts clearly.

articles.headline: Meta narrows click-through attribution and forces teams to reset the story they tell.
articles.summary: Meta is redefining click-through attribution for website and in-store conversions around actual link clicks, while moving social interactions into engage-through views.

articles.headline: TikTok is becoming more operationally integrated across paid, creator, commerce, and profile governance.
articles.summary: Recent product updates expand Smart+, Market Scope, and creator workflows, while profile-based identity changes make owned presence more important.

briefings.headline: Company-level attribution strengthens the B2B story.
briefings.summary: LinkedIn’s Company Intelligence API gives marketers a better way to discuss account-level influence and buying-group engagement.

briefings.headline: Top of Search pushes Pinterest further into demand capture.
briefings.summary: Pinterest is getting more useful for lower-funnel, high-intent moments, especially when paired with clean shopping feeds.

briefings.headline: Reddit automation expands, but community context still matters.
briefings.summary: Max campaigns make scaling easier, but operators should still test whether community-native planning outperforms automation.
